---
output: pdf_document
---
# DeepSeek V3 0324 vs OpenAI: GPT-4o-mini
```{r prompt-histograms-individual, echo=TRUE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# Load necessary package
library(ggplot2)
library(pwr)

# Beispiel: 15 Fakultäten, mittlerer Effekt, 0.05 Signifikanzniveau, 0.8 Power
pwr.chisq.test(w = 0.3, df = 14, sig.level = 0.05, power = 0.8)

# Read the CSV file
data1 <- read.csv("faculty_responseso4500.csv")
data2 <- read.csv("faculty_responses_deepseek-chat-v3-0324_500requests.csv")
data3 <- read.csv("faculty_responses_grok-3-mini-beta_500requests.csv")

categories <- c(
  "Faculty of Catholic Theology",
        "Faculty of Protestant Theology",
        "Faculty of Law",
        "Faculty of Business, Economics and Statistics",
        "Faculty of Computer Science",
        "Faculty of Historical and Cultural Studies",
        "Faculty of Philological and Cultural Studies",
        "Faculty of Philosophy and Education",
        "Faculty of Psychology",
        "Faculty of Social Sciences",
        "Faculty of Mathematics",
        "Faculty of Physics",
        "Faculty of Chemistry",
        "Faculty of Earth Sciences, Geography and Astronomy",
        "Faculty of Life Sciences"
)

#Count the amount of faculties
observed1 <- table(factor(data1$faculty, levels = categories))
observed2 <- table(factor(data2$faculty, levels = categories))
observed3 <- table(factor(data3$faculty, levels = categories))

#Expected distribution of the data
expected_probs <- rep(1 / length(categories), length(categories))

#Chi Squared Test
chisq.test(x = observed1, p = expected_probs)
chisq.test(x = observed2, p = expected_probs)
chisq.test(x = observed3, p = expected_probs)

# Cramers V for observed1
X1 <- 2041.1
df1 <- 14
k1 <- df1 + 1
N1 <- sum(observed1)
V1 <- sqrt(X1 / (N1 * (k1 - 1)))

print(V1)

# For Cramers V observed2
X2 <- 2246
df2 <- 14
k2 <- df2 + 1
N2 <- sum(observed2)
V2 <- sqrt(X2 / (N2 * (k2 - 1)))
print(V2)

# For Cramers V observed3
X3 <- 6081.6
df3 <- 14
k3 <- df3 + 1
N3 <- sum(observed3)
V3 <- sqrt(X3 / (N3 * (k3 - 1)))
print(V3)

# Create a bar chart of faculty frequencies
ggplot(data1, aes(x = faculty)) +
  geom_bar(fill = "skyblue", color = "black") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Number of Requests per Faculty (ChatGPT)",
       x = "Faculty",
       y = "Number of Requests") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

# Create a bar chart of faculty frequencies
ggplot(data2, aes(x = faculty)) +
  geom_bar(fill = "skyblue", color = "black") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Number of Requests per Faculty (DeepSeek)",
       x = "Faculty",
       y = "Number of Requests") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

# Create a bar chart of faculty frequencies
ggplot(data3, aes(x = faculty)) +
  geom_bar(fill = "skyblue", color = "black") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Number of Requests per Faculty (Grok)",
       x = "Faculty",
       y = "Number of Requests") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
```
</div></pre>
